explanation: Statement 1 is correct because India's bullet train project is indeed inspired by Japan's Shinkansen technology. Statement 2 is correct as the first operational corridor is planned between Ahmedabad and Mumbai. Statement 3 is incorrect because the project is being developed with Japanese assistance, not Chinese.

Shinkansen (Japan), Maglev (Germany/China), and Eurostar (UK/France) are all types of high-speed or advanced train systems. Talgo is a Spanish train manufacturer known for its conventional and high-speed trains, but it is not a distinct type of bullet train technology in the same category as the others.

While China has a very extensive and advanced high-speed rail network, it is not definitively the fastest, most advanced, or most expensive in the world across all metrics. Other countries also operate high-speed trains that compete in these categories, and definitive rankings can vary based on specific criteria.
